title: The 50 Best Creator Posts About Pittsburgh Businesses
subtitle: TikToks made by Pittsburgh creators, ranked by plays. None of these came from the businesses themselves.
angle: the city is filming Pittsburgh businesses; the businesses are not posting back. Here's the proof, ranked.
intro:
Twenty-four and a half million plays, 49 creators, 14 businesses. Not one of those videos came from the businesses themselves.

That is the story of Pittsburgh on TikTok right now. Locals are filming the city's small businesses with a consistency and reach that no in-house marketing budget could match. @joshua.guitar's video on Everyday Noodles in Squirrel Hill North has 11.4 million plays. @boburtzz followed with 4.5 million more on the same restaurant. @amalkzad turned a Bloomfield juice counter, 24 Carrot Juice, into a skincare argument with 671,000 plays. The businesses in this list are not coordinating any of this. They are not posting back. They are simply the subject.

The pattern holds across every entry in our top 50. Creators found these places on their own, framed them on their own terms, and built audiences the restaurants and shops have not yet figured out how to inherit. Whether that gap closes, or widens, is what we will be watching when we return with Issue 02 this summer.
